result: If I could change the world in one way, I would try to make empathy a universal, lived reality—so that every person felt a genuine, visceral connection to the well‑being of every other person. Not just as a vague ideal, but as a deep‑seated habit that shapes decisions at every level, from personal relationships to global policy. Imagine a world where the suffering of a stranger on the other side of the planet carries the same moral weight as the suffering of a loved one, where resource allocation, environmental action, and social justice are all guided by an unwavering sense of shared humanity. That shift in collective feeling could, I think, address most of the other crises we face—poverty, conflict, climate change—because it would transform the underlying motivation behind how we treat each other and the planet.
